description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Hiroshima Prefecture (広島県, Hiroshima-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [çi.ɾo.ɕi.ma, -maꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Chūgoku region of Honshu.

description: conflict between the Russian and Japanese empires from 1904 to 1905
description_wiki: The Russo-Japanese War (8 February 1904 – 5 September 1905) was fought between the Russian Empire and the Empire of Japan over rival imperial ambitions in Manchuria and the Korean Empire.

description: designated city in Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Yokohama (Japanese: 横浜; pronounced [jokohama] ) is the second-largest city in Japan by population as well as by area, and the country's most populous municipality.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Hawaii (  hə-WY-ee; Hawaiian: Hawaiʻi [həˈvɐjʔi, həˈwɐjʔi]) is an island state of the United States, in the Pacific Ocean about 2,000 miles (3,200 km) southwest of the U.S. mainland.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

aliases: Great San Francisco Earthquake and Fire
description: major earthquake that struck San Francisco and the coast of Northern California
description_wiki: The 1906 San Francisco earthquake was a major earthquake which struck the coast of Northern California at 05:12 am Pacific Standard Time on Wednesday, April 18, 1906.

description: census-designated place in Sacramento County, California, United States
description_wiki: Walnut Grove is a census-designated place (CDP) in Sacramento County, California, United States.

description: city in San Joaquin County, California, United States
description_wiki: Lodi ( LOH-dye) is a city in San Joaquin County, California, United States, in the center portion of California's Central Valley.

description: city and county seat in Yuba County, California, United States
description_wiki: Marysville is a city in and the county seat of Yuba County, California, located in the Gold Country region of Northern California.

description: construction company founded in 1900
description_wiki: The Utah Construction Company was a construction company founded by Edmund Orson Wattis Jr., Warren L. Wattis and William.

description: capital city of the U.S. state of California and seat of Sacramento County
description_wiki: Sacramento is the capital city of the U.S. state of California.

description: city in Fresno County, California, United States
description_wiki: Reedley is a city in Fresno County, California, United States.

description: city in Fresno County, California, United States
description_wiki: Clovis is a city in Fresno County, California, United States.

description: given name and family name
description_wiki: Gerhardt is a masculine name of German origin.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: fair ground in Fresno, California, USA
state highway in California, United States
description_wiki: State Route 99 (SR 99) is a major north–south state highway in the U.S. state of California, stretching almost the entire length of the Central Valley.

description: ethnic group native to China Central Plain
description_wiki: The Han Chinese, alternatively Han people, or Chinese people, are an East Asian ethnic group native to Greater China.

description: people based in the Korean Peninsula and Manchuria
people native to or citizens of the Philippines
description_wiki: Filipinos (Filipino: Mga Pilipino) are citizens or people identified and related with the country or sovereign state of the Philippines.

aliases: World War II Japanese American Internment Museum
description: history museum in McGehee, Arkansas
Japanese American internment camp
description_wiki: The Jerome War Relocation Center was a Japanese American internment camp located in southeastern Arkansas, near the town of Jerome in the Arkansas Delta.

description: consolidated city-county and capital of Colorado, United States
description_wiki: Denver  (  DEN-vər) is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Colorado.

description: second generation of Japanese immigrants
description_wiki: Nisei (二世, "second generation") is a Japanese-language term used in countries in North America and South America to specify the ethnically Japanese children born in the new country to Japanese-born immigrants, or Issei.

description: modern martial art, combat and Olympic/Paralympic sport
description_wiki: Judo (Japanese: 柔道, Hepburn: Jūdō; lit. 'gentle way') is an unarmed modern Japanese martial art, combat sport, Olympic sport (since 1964), Paralympic sport (since 1988) and Commonwealth Games sport (since 1990).

description: modern Japanese martial art
description_wiki: Kendo (Japanese: 剣道, Hepburn: Kendō; lit. 'sword way'  or 'sword path'  or 'way of the sword') is a modern Japanese martial art, descended from kenjutsu (one of the old Japanese martial arts, swordsmanship), that uses bamboo swords (shinai) as well as protective armor (bōgu).

description: school that teaches the Japanese language to non-native speakers
Japanese actor and seiyū
